title: ARC Analyst Handbook: RBI Asset Reconstruction Companies Directions, 2025
stale_items: The legacy-ARC NOF glide path deadline (₹300 crore by 31 March 2026 for ARCs existing as on 11 October 2022) has already lapsed as of July 2026 — treat any ARC still below ₹300 crore NOF as non-compliant now, not 'on a glide path'.
executive_summary: 20-chapter reference handbook distilling the RBI (Asset Reconstruction Companies) Directions, 2025 (DoR.FIN.REC.No.293/26.03.001/2025-26, dated 28 Nov 2025, updated to 10 Mar 2026); ARCs are regulated under SARFAESI (Sections 3, 9, 10, 12, 12A), not the RBI Act, and a single CoR covers both securitisation and asset reconstruction.

Capital gateway: minimum NOF of ₹300 crore (ongoing; legacy ARCs had until 31 Mar 2026), with a March 2026 amendment letting quarterly profits count toward Owned Fund via EPt = NPt − 0.25×D×t; the IBC Resolution Applicant role needs ₹1,000 crore NOF plus a 5-year control-exit sunset; deposit-taking is absolutely prohibited and bilateral acquisitions from sponsors/lenders/group entities are banned (open auctions permitted).

Acquisition and realisation mechanics: Board-approved acquisition policy within 90 days of CoR, at least 2 weeks pre-bid diligence, a 60%-by-value secured-creditor consent threshold for enforcement, a 6-month Planning Period (assets treated as standard), and a 5-year base / 8-year maximum realisation ceiling that carries over unreset even on ARC-to-ARC secondary sales.

Reconstruction toolkit: management change/takeover needs the ARC's claim ≥25% of borrower total assets plus 60% SR-holder consent, IAC review, 60-day notice and a reasoned Board order in 30 days; settlements above ₹1 crore require IAC-plus-independent-director committee governance (fraud/wilful-defaulter cases regardless of size, with a Para 62 NPV-≥-realisable-security-value floor); debt-to-equity conversion is capped at 26% of post-conversion equity unless a four-condition exemption (half-independent Board, majority-ID committee, monthly MTM, NOF compliance) is met.

Security Receipts: privately placed, QB-only, generally below-investment-grade hybrids issued via ARC-trusteed trusts; the ARC must retain the higher of 15% of transferors' SR investment or 2.5% of total SRs per class until full redemption; recovery ratings (a recovery-percentage band, not a default rating) are due within 6 months of acquisition, reviewed each 30 June/31 December with 6-cycle (3-year) minimum CRA retention; NAV = face value × the ARC's chosen point within the CRA band (judgement risk).

Prudential rules: single CRAR of NOF/RWA ≥15% with a flat 100% risk weight on virtually all core assets (0% only on cash/G-secs/other-ARC shares, 50% on contingents); the ARC's own NPA clock is 180 days (vs 90 for NBFCs) with sub-standard ≤12 months, doubtful thereafter, and automatic Loss at 36 months NPA or on breach of the 5/8-year realisation ceiling; provisioning is security-coverage-driven — 10% sub-standard, 100% on the uncovered doubtful portion plus 50% on the covered portion, 100%/write-off for Loss, and no standard-asset provision.

Earnings quality is deliberately suppressed: SR yield/upside is recognised only after full redemption of the entire SR principal (no accrual), and management fee — computed on NAV at the lower end of the CRA range, capped at acquisition value, payable only from recoveries — must be reversed if unrealised within 180 days or immediately if SR NAV falls below 50% of face value, with unrealised fee deducted from NOF for Ind-AS ARCs; ECL runs against an IRACP prudential floor via a non-distributable Impairment Reserve.

Governance and disclosure: prior RBI approval for every director/MD-CEO (90-day PRAVAAH lead time), mandatory independent Board Chair for all ARCs, MD/CEO caps of 70 years age / 5-year terms / 15-year continuous tenure with 3-year cooling-off, sponsor fit-and-proper with FATF-jurisdiction investors capped below 20% voting power; the Para 150 schedule mandates 18 balance-sheet disclosures (including auto-flags for >20% disposal discounts and >20% SR value declines), plus monthly public SARFAESI-possession disclosure and an FPC with 8am–7pm recovery calling hours and ₹5,000/day document-release delay compensation.

key_takeaways.takeaway: An ARC below ₹300 crore NOF faces prohibition on incremental business — existential for a pure-recovery model — and the quarterly-profit inclusion route (EPt = NPt − 0.25×D×t, March 2026 amendment) makes Owned Fund composition more volatile and audit-dependent.
key_takeaways.analyst_lens: NOF is the single capital gateway (no SBR-style layers) and cross-holdings in group entities or other ARCs beyond 10% of Owned Fund are netted out, so a strong headline Owned Fund can collapse into a weak NOF. Track NOF trend versus ₹300 crore and the share of NOF resting on quarterly profits rather than paid-up capital.

key_takeaways.takeaway: The 5-year base / 8-year maximum realisation ceiling is a forcing function: any asset (including via ARC-to-ARC sale, which inherits the original clock) unrealised past the ceiling is automatically classified Loss and fully provided/written off regardless of collateral quality.
key_takeaways.analyst_lens: A book with heavy vintages approaching year 5-8 faces a wall of compulsory write-offs even on genuinely recoverable assets; routine Board extensions to 8 years also delay QBs' Section 7(3) enforcement rights. Track the vintage distribution of acquired assets against the 5/8-year clock and the share of Loss classifications that are timeline-driven versus collateral-driven.

key_takeaways.takeaway: ARC reported earnings structurally lag economic recovery: SR yield/upside is recognised only after full redemption of the entire SR principal, and management fee reverses if unrealised in 180 days or instantly when SR NAV drops below 50% of face value — with unrealised fee deducted from NOF for Ind-AS ARCs.
key_takeaways.analyst_lens: A young ARC can show thin profits while building real recovery value, and conversely a fee-heavy P&L can be hollow if collections lag. Track the Para 150(18) unrealised management-fee ageing (especially >1-year and >3-year buckets) and scheme-level collection efficiency against realisation plans, not just reported P&L.

key_takeaways.takeaway: Capital adequacy is mechanically fragile: a single 15% CRAR test on NOF with a flat 100% risk weight on essentially the whole core asset base means CRAR compresses near-1:1 with book growth and offers almost no risk-weight-mix management lever.
key_takeaways.analyst_lens: Growth is capital-intensive and CRAR tells you about capital, not asset quality (unlike NBFCs where RW mix encodes quality). Track CRAR headroom above 15% against planned acquisition volume, plus NOF-eroding items: provisioning hits, unrealised-fee deductions, and fair-value losses (gains are excluded, losses included — asymmetric).

key_takeaways.takeaway: SR recovery ratings are not credit ratings and NAV embeds management judgement: NAV = face value × a percentage the ARC itself selects within a ±10pp-wide CRA recovery band, reviewed only half-yearly (30 June/31 December).
key_takeaways.analyst_lens: For any lender holding SRs, published NAV is a judgement-laden, lagging number — two ARCs with identical CRA bands can report different NAVs. Track recovery-rating migration across half-yearly reviews and whether the ARC consistently picks the top of its band without realised-collection support; CRA changes inside the mandatory 6-cycle (3-year) retention window are a rating-shopping red flag.

key_takeaways.takeaway: Doubtful-asset provisioning swings entirely on the 'estimated realisable value of security' — 100% on the uncovered portion plus 50% on the covered portion — making collateral valuation quality a live provisioning input every period, not just an acquisition-stage control.
key_takeaways.analyst_lens: An ARC carrying stale or optimistic realisable-value assumptions can materially understate provisions (e.g., ₹100 cr doubtful at 60% coverage needs ₹70 cr provision; at 30% coverage, ₹85 cr). Track provision coverage ratio trend, disclosed covered/uncovered splits, and revaluation recency on large doubtful exposures.

key_takeaways.takeaway: The 180-day regulatory NPA clock (twice the NBFC 90-day standard) coexists with Ind-AS 109's 90-day default presumption, so Ind-AS ARCs generally recognise Stage 3 impairment earlier than regulatory NPA — any later default definition needs ACB approval and disclosure, and SICR recognition cannot be deferred beyond 60 days past due.
key_takeaways.analyst_lens: The accounting-versus-regulatory gap is a disclosure-rich diagnostic: the Annex VI ECL-vs-IRACP comparison and the Impairment Reserve (non-distributable, non-capital) reveal whether ECL models are running lighter than the prudential floor. Track the size and trend of Impairment Reserve appropriations and disclosed >90-day-not-impaired accounts.

key_takeaways.takeaway: Related-party and captive-vehicle risk is the framework's central anti-abuse theme: bilateral acquisitions from sponsor banks, ARC lenders/fund subscribers and group entities are banned (auctions only), management fee may be paid only out of actual recoveries, and enforcement-acquired land/buildings must be disposed of within 5 years.
key_takeaways.analyst_lens: Historic ARC failure modes are sponsor-captive balance-sheet laundering and becoming an accidental real-estate/PE vehicle. Track Para 150 Item 1 seller concentration (dominance of a single sponsor bank even via 'auctions' warrants auction-design scrutiny), year-wise land/building holdings against the 5-year window, and debt-conversion equity stakes as a percentage of NOF.

key_takeaways.takeaway: Governance requirements exceed the NBFC middle-layer standard — universal independent Board Chair, prior RBI approval for every director appointment, 70-year age cap, 5-year terms with a 15-year continuous-tenure ceiling and 3-year cooling-off, and NRC scrutiny extending to sponsors — because ARC economics rest on hard-to-verify judgement calls (NAV selection, settlement NPV, takeover grounds).
key_takeaways.analyst_lens: Governance quality is the most reliable differentiator between ARCs with similar headline metrics. Track independent-chair genuineness, MD/CEO tenure against the 15-year ceiling with succession planning, IAC member independence, and whether fraud/wilful-defaulter settlements get full Tier-1 committee governance irrespective of size.

key_takeaways.takeaway: The Para 150 schedule of 18 mandatory balance-sheet disclosures is the richest single diagnostic on any ARC, including automatic threshold-triggered flags: assets disposed at >20% discount to prior-year valuation and SRs whose value has declined >20% below acquisition value.
key_takeaways.analyst_lens: Read items together: acquisition (Item 5) vs realisation (Item 6) vs backlog (Item 7) trended over 3+ years reveals whether the pipeline is outrunning recovery capacity; Items 13-14 surface material valuation misses without digging; monthly public SARFAESI-possession disclosure on the ARC's website is a free primary-source cross-check on specific exposures.

entities_impacted.impact: Issuer of the ARC Directions 2025 under SARFAESI; grants CoR, approves every director appointment, caps FATF-jurisdiction ownership, and can prohibit incremental business for NOF non-compliance.

Directly bound by the full framework: ₹300 crore NOF, 15% CRAR at flat 100% risk weights, 5/8-year realisation ceilings, conservative income recognition, and strict governance/conduct rules — post-2022 consolidation pressure favours fewer, better-capitalised players.

entities_impacted.entity: Banks and NBFCs selling stressed assets / holding Security Receipts
entities_impacted.impact: SR valuation, income recognition and risk on their balance sheets depend on ARC-side rules — recovery-rating bands, ARC-chosen NAV points, the ARC's 15%/2.5% retention, and the 10% holding threshold for stricter notional-provisioning treatment under the Transfer framework.

Sole permitted holders of privately placed SRs; protected by the 25% cap on scheme funds used for restructuring, quarterly disclosures, and Section 7(3) enforcement rights that activate only at the end of any Board-extended realisation period.

entities_impacted.entity: Credit Rating Agencies assigning recovery ratings
entities_impacted.impact: Must rate SRs within 6 months of acquisition and half-yearly thereafter with a minimum 6-cycle engagement; ARC-CRA commonality/conflict must be disclosed quarterly, and mid-window CRA changes require disclosed reasons to deter rating shopping.

Subject to management change/takeover on 12 grounds (with 25%-of-assets and 60%-SR-consent eligibility tests, 60-day notice, IAC due process), debt-to-equity conversion up to 26% (or more via exemption), and FPC protections including 8am-7pm recovery calling hours and 30-day document release.

learning: For ARCs, always check the realisation-timeline clock before collateral analysis: the 5/8-year ceiling silently drives mandatory Loss classification — the most severe consequence in the framework — and secondary ARC-to-ARC sales inherit, not reset, the original 8-year clock.

Treat SR NAV and recovery ratings as judgement-laden estimates, not observed prices: NAV is the ARC's chosen point inside a ±10pp CRA band updated only half-yearly, so build an independent recovery model for any material SR exposure and track band-selection behaviour across schemes.

ARC earnings analysis inverts NBFC analysis: with SR yield locked until full redemption and management fee on an aggressive 180-day/NAV-below-50% reversal regime, judge the franchise on scheme-level collection efficiency against realisation plans, not reported P&L.

An ARC's CRAR carries no asset-quality information — the flat 100% risk weight makes RWA nearly proportional to gross book size — so asset quality must be read separately through sub-standard-to-doubtful migration rates and security-coverage-driven provisioning adequacy.
